metadata.title:
CONTINU ED

FROM SWEAT BOX


TO REFRIGERATOR

pages.raw_text:
The revolutionists do not look
with favor, to say the least, upon a
plan to permit street speaking only
in certain districts, and it is plan
of this kind that is advanced by
the delegates to the Central Labor
union, who appointed a committee
to meet the city council tonight,
with a view to having the trouble
some ordinance modified.


Under no conditions will we con-
slider a proposition of this kind, or
any other kind, without that an I'M
conditional surrender of all our
men is made by the police, to begin
with" one of the |. w. w. members
told a reporter of The Press this
morning. with surrender of our
men, we will be willing to meet the
city officials half way, and we will
at least consider their propositions
for a compromise"


More Men Arriving.


More men are arriving from vari-
Gus sections of the country today
some coming from Nevada, some
from Montana, others from Oregon
and many are just arriving from
Seattle. Money is still coming in,
and from present circumstances
there is yet no indication of an
abatement of the fight.


Woman Carried on Stretcher.


Miss Agnes Fair, one of the wo-
men revolutionists taken to the city
Jail Saturday, for speaking on the
streets, was carried on a stretcher
from the city Jail last night by her
fellow workers" She is still in a
weak condition at the st. Louis
rooming house today, as the result
of her participation in the hunger
strike" and because of alleged i'll
treatment at the hands of the de-
tectives.


About a dozen more men were
arrested this morning.
